items.title: Blocked lanes remain blocked until their named contract gap is resolved
items.rule: If an issue has blocked dependencies, missing contract, implementation action class, or high drift, dry-run must return no_op and must not move the lane to in_review.

items.title: Scheduled sweep command scope is explicit and narrow
items.rule: Scheduled governance reporters may use only targeted board/status commands declared in manifest tool scope; broad environment probing and help/discovery commands are forbidden.
items.next_externalization_candidate: Add a command-policy validator before any scheduled live executor exists.

items.title: Grade-C red-team fallback cannot clear high-risk blockers alone
items.rule: If reviewer independence grade is C or D and the issue is high risk, the gate may produce critique but cannot unblock implementation or live automation.

items.title: No-op is a valid successful sweep outcome
items.rule: If no candidate satisfies all checks, the correct output is no_op with failed checks and drift reasons; creating an issue is not progress by itself.

items.title: High MCP or skills drift blocks transition proposals
items.rule: If live snapshot reports high or critical drift, transition proposal must be blocked even when individual issue data looks eligible.

items.title: Live issue defaults are conservative until issue contracts are machine-readable
items.rule: Live-exported issues default to implementation/high-risk/contract-incomplete/red-team-not-started so no live issue can pass accidentally.
items.next_externalization_candidate: Replace conservative defaults only after Multica issues carry machine-readable contract metadata.

items.title: Cross-workspace evidence is not transparent enough for red-team by default
items.rule: When a review issue lives in assistant-ops and target issue lives in assistant-factory, the contract and artifact refs must be copied into the review issue until a cross-workspace evidence bridge exists.
items.next_externalization_candidate: Implement a cross-workspace evidence bridge that attaches target issue snapshots to red-team tasks.

items.title: User approval boundary for authority expansion and direction changes
items.rule: Chief, workers, and sidecar may not decide product-direction changes, secret expansion, permission expansion, financial production behavior, or external user-facing actions without explicit user approval.
items.next_externalization_candidate: Add issue risk taxonomy fields so the checker can detect authority-expansion classes automatically.

items.title: Whether a deferred architecture choice is direction-level
items.rule: When evidence is complete but the decision changes the product architecture or operating model, sidecar must summarize tradeoffs and ask the user rather than converting it into a machine gate.
items.human_only_rationale: Direction-level product and architecture choices require user intent, not just evidence completeness.
items.next_externalization_candidate: Define a small direction-change taxonomy so obvious cases can be auto-escalated without sidecar interpretation.

items.title: Whether a Pro/GAC recommendation misunderstood local context
items.rule: If external review conflicts with verified local state, sidecar must either supply more context and re-ask or record a reasoned override; it cannot silently follow the review.
items.human_only_rationale: This is a meta-review judgment about context sufficiency and cannot be safely reduced to a field check yet.
items.next_externalization_candidate: Track missing-evidence items and require a documented resolution field before accepting conditional reviews.
